data.title: ChargePoint achieves positive revenue growth and narrows Q3 loss on subscription momentum
data.description:
After several uneven quarters with decelerating revenue, ChargePoint (CHPT) reported a solid third quarter, narrowing its loss and achieving 6% revenue growth to a better-than-expected $106.7M.

“ChargePoint is showing early signs of stabilization,” said Needham analyst Chris Pierce in response to Q3 results. “Delivering a beat with in-line guidance, continued subscription momentum, improving cost discipline, and a meaningfully strengthened balance sheet,” following its debt exchange with Eaton.

Revenue growth for the quarter was fueled by a 15% increase in subscription revenue and 7% gain in networked charging systems revenue.

Profitability improved as well with ChargePoint (CHPT) reporting an unadjusted loss of $2.23 per share versus a loss of $3.56 a year ago, although 7 cents below expectations. Additionally, on the increase in subscription revenue and subscription margins, adjusted gross margin improved 700 basis points to a record high of 33%.

The company also issued Q4 revenue guidance that was better-than-expected with a range of $100M to $110M with a midpoint of $105M that is above the $102.9M estimate.

“ChargePoint’s expanding partnerships (namely Eaton) and new platform launch positions the company to capture the [European EV adoption] momentum,” Pierce said, adding that “bulls can argue that the worst of the reset is behind the company, setting the stage for CHPT to re-emerge as leverage returns to even modest demand recovery.”

Agrees Roth’s Craig Irwin. “The debt refinancing was a clear positive, and now ChargePoint needs to take more aggressive action targeting adjusted EBITDA breakeven.”

On the company’s earnings call with analysts, CFO Mansi Khetani acknowledged the focus on positive EBITDA and noted that “EBITDA gains will come with growth in revenue which we are significantly focused on. With the new products and the increased demand in Europe and the Eaton partnership, we think the second half [of the year] should be pretty strong.”

ChargePoint (CHPT) shares lurched higher in Thursday’s after hours trading and are now up 10% ahead of Friday’s open.

data.title: ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund announces balance sheet information
data.description:
• ClearBridge Energy Midstream Opportunity Fund (EMO) announced on Friday the unaudited statement of assets and liabilities, the net asset value and asset coverage ratio of the fund as of November 30, 2025.
  • As of November 30, 2025, the fund's net assets were $978.4M, and its net asset value per share was $48.88. 
  • The fund’s asset coverage ratio regarding senior indebtedness was 791%, and the fund's asset coverage ratio with respect to total leverage was 545%.

data.title: Soluna Holdings announces $32M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Soluna Holdings (SLNH) entered into definitive agreements to sell 18.08M shares (or pre-funded warrants) plus Series C warrants in a registered direct offering priced at-the-market.
  • The offering is priced at $1.77/share (or pre-funded warrant) with Series C warrants exercisable at $1.65/share for five years.
  • The offering is expected to generate ~$32M in gross proceeds.
  • Soluna plans to use the net proceeds for working capital, project-level equity needs, and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is anticipated to close on or about December 5, 2025.
  • SLNH shares down 3% premarket on Friday.
